posts.content: 1769年8月15日，我在阿雅克肖出世——科西嘉并入法国刚满一年，岛屿与大国的张力塑造了我的野心。
posts.translation: On 15 August 1769 I was born in Ajaccio—Corsica had joined France only a year before; island pride against great-power politics shaped my ambition.

posts.content: 1779年我入布里埃纳军校——在法国军官堆里，我用地图与数学证明自己。
posts.translation: In 1779 I entered the Brienne military academy—among French cadets I proved myself with maps and mathematics.

posts.content: 1793年土伦之战，我以炮兵少校之身攻城——功名从此不再是空想。
posts.translation: At Toulon in 1793 I took the fortifications as an artillery captain—glory stopped being an abstraction.

posts.content: 1796年我领军进入意大利，阿科莱与里沃利证明——速度与集中兵力胜过数量。
posts.translation: In 1796 I drove into Italy—Arcola and Rivoli taught Europe that speed and mass beat mere numbers.

posts.content: 1798年5月我率东方军团驶向埃及——我要切断英国往印度的臂膀，也要把科学与考古带回巴黎。
posts.translation: In May 1798 I sailed for Egypt—I meant to cut Britain’s route east and ship science and antiquity back to Paris.

posts.content: 雾月十八日我驱散五百人院——执政府的混乱告终，我以第一执政收拾法兰西。
posts.translation: On 18 Brumaire I cleared the Council of Five Hundred—the Directorate’s chaos ended; as First Consul I stitched France together.

posts.content: 马伦戈一战我险中求胜——奥地利被迫谈判，意大利再度臣服。
posts.translation: Marengo was won at the edge of disaster—Austria sued for peace and Italy bent again.

posts.content: 1804年12月2日巴黎圣母院加冕——我从教皇手中拿过皇冠，自己戴在头上。
posts.translation: On 2 December 1804 I crowned myself at Notre-Dame—I lifted the crown from the pontiff’s hands and placed it myself.

posts.content: 奥斯特利茨的太阳升起时，第三次反法同盟崩溃——俄奥联军在我的中央突破下瓦解。
posts.translation: When the sun rose at Austerlitz the Third Coalition shattered—the Russo-Austrian line broke on my center strike.

posts.content: 耶拿与奥尔施泰特一日之间击溃普鲁士——中欧版图重新洗牌。
posts.translation: Jena and Auerstedt smashed Prussia in a day—I redrew Central Europe at marching speed.

posts.content: 九月我进入莫斯科空城，继而寒冬与焦土耗尽大军——我开始明白帝国的边界。
posts.translation: In September I entered an empty Moscow—winter and scorched earth drained my host; I learned the empire’s outer limit.

posts.content: 1814年4月我首次逊位，流放厄尔巴——法兰西的钟声仍在耳边。
posts.translation: In April 1814 I abdicated first to Elba—France’s bells still rang in my ears.

posts.content: 滑铁卢的雨季终结百日王朝——威灵顿与布吕歇尔联手将我推向最后的岛。
posts.translation: Waterloo’s mud ended the Hundred Days—Wellington and Blücher drove me to my final island.

posts.content: 1821年5月5日我在圣赫勒拿咽下最后一口气——地图缩小成南大西洋的一块岩石。
posts.translation: On 5 May 1821 I drew my last breath at Saint Helena—the map shrank to a rock in the South Atlantic.
